senses.definition: A misspelling or variant spelling of pyloric stenosis, a narrowing of the pylorus that blocks food from leaving the stomach.
examples: The report listed pyloristenosis, but the doctor said pyloric stenosis was intended.
Symptoms like vomiting can happen when the pylorus is narrowed (pyloristenosis).

senses.definition: Inflammation of the pylorus, the opening at the lower part of the stomach.
examples: The patient was diagnosed with pyloritis after stomach pain and nausea.
Pyloritis can make it harder for food to move from the stomach to the intestine.

senses.definition: A medical term for closure or blockage of the pylorus.
examples: The clinician used pylorocleisis to describe a blocked pyloric opening.
Pylorocleisis can cause food to back up in the stomach.

senses.definition: A substance or treatment that dilates (widens) the pylorus.
examples: The doctor described a pylorodilator to help widen the pyloric opening.
A pylorodilator may improve how food leaves the stomach.

senses.definition: Surgery that removes part of the pylorus and part of the stomach (gastrectomy involving the pyloric region).
examples: Pylorogastrectomy was recommended due to disease near the pylorus.
After a pylorogastrectomy, patients may need a special diet.

senses.definition: Surgery that widens or reshapes the pylorus to help stomach contents pass into the small intestine.
examples: The patient had pyloroplasty to improve gastric emptying.
After pyloroplasty, food moved more easily from the stomach to the duodenum.

senses.definition: A condition where the pylorus is displaced or droops (a form of prolapse or downward movement involving the pylorus).
examples: The diagnosis included pyloroptosis after imaging showed displacement of the pyloric area.
Pyloroptosis may contribute to problems with stomach emptying.

senses.definition: A medical term for a condition involving the pylorus; likely intended to mean a functional disorder or contraction-like problem in the pyloric area.
examples: The note mentioned pyloroschesis, described as a pyloric-function problem.
Pyloroschesis was considered when symptoms suggested pyloric dysfunction.

senses.definition: A medical term suggesting hardening or scarring (fibrosis) around the pylorus.
examples: Pyloroscirrhus can describe scarring that makes the pylorus less flexible.
The doctor explained that pyloroscirrhus may narrow the stomach outlet.

senses.definition: A misspelling or incorrect form related to pyloroscopy, an examination of the pylorus.
examples: The medical text seems to contain a typo: pyloroscopys was likely pyloroscopy.
Pyloroscopy helps doctors look at the pylorus area.

senses.definition: An endoscopic examination of the pylorus, the opening from the stomach into the small intestine.
examples: The doctor planned a pyloroscopy to check the pylorus.
Pyloroscopy may help find causes of stomach-outlet problems.

senses.definition: A spasm (sudden tightening) of the pylorus that can interfere with food moving from the stomach.
examples: Pylorospasm can cause nausea because the stomach cannot empty well.
After treatment, the pylorospasm improved and symptoms reduced.

senses.definition: A misspelling or variant spelling of pyloric stenosis, meaning narrowing of the pylorus that blocks stomach contents.
examples: The diagnosis was written as pylorostenosis, but it referred to pyloric stenosis.
Narrowing of the pylorus can lead to forceful vomiting.

senses.definition: Surgery that creates an opening in the pylorus (or near it) to allow stomach contents to pass.
examples: Pylorostomy was performed to help food move from the stomach into the intestine.
After pylorostomy, the doctor monitored how well the new opening worked.
collocations: create an opening in the pylorus

senses.definition: Relating to the pylorus, the opening between the stomach and the small intestine.
examples: The pylorous region showed swelling in the exam.
Doctors described pylorous function tests.

senses.definition: A likely incorrect or uncommon form related to pylorus; it is not a standard contemporary English word.
examples: The term pylorouses does not look standard; it may be a typo for pylorus or pyloruses.
If you can share the original sentence, the intended meaning can be identified.

senses.definition: The opening at the lower part of the stomach that lets food pass into the small intestine.
examples: Food passes through the pylorus and enters the duodenum.
Pylorus problems can cause trouble emptying the stomach.

senses.definition: Plural form referring to more than one pylorus (the openings between the stomach and small intestine).
examples: In some descriptions, pyloruses are mentioned when comparing anatomy between specimens.
The report refers to two pyloruses seen during examination.

senses.definition: An old word for a young person; also used as a surname.
examples: Pyne is a surname found in many English-speaking countries.
In older texts, pyne could refer to a young person.

senses.definition: A misspelling or rare/archaic form; likely intended to be ‘pined,’ meaning to feel strong longing or sadness for something.
examples: The sentence seems to mean pined, not pyned.
She pyned for home for weeks.

senses.definition: A misspelling or uncommon variant of ‘pines’ (third-person singular), meaning feels longing or sadness for something.
examples: The text likely intended pines: he pynes for his childhood friend.
She pynes quietly when the letters stop coming.

senses.definition: A misspelling or variant of ‘pining,’ meaning feeling strong longing or sadness for someone or something.
examples: He was pyning for the life he used to have.
The poem uses pyning (likely pining) to show deep longing.

senses.definition: A likely misspelling or uncommon form related to ‘pynot,’ not a standard contemporary English word; exact meaning depends on context (possibly a name or typo).
examples: Pynot appears to be a misspelling; it might mean a proper name or another word.
Can you share the full sentence with pynot?

senses.definition: Not a standard contemporary English word; likely a misspelling/variant related to ‘pining’ or another form.
examples: Pynung is probably meant to be pining or another similar word.
Without context, the intended meaning of pynung is unclear.

senses.definition: A disease/condition caused by bacteria of the genus Pyo-bacillus (a specialized medical term used in some classifications).
examples: The veterinarian mentioned pyobacillosis as a possible cause of infection.
Pyobacillosis can be treated with appropriate antibiotics.

senses.definition: A collection of pus (infected fluid) in a body cavity, often described in medical contexts.
examples: The scan showed a pyocele that needed medical treatment.
A pyocele is an accumulation of pus in a specific area.
